2603.15901.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Medical AI
2603.15901.paper_summary: This dissertation investigates privacy-preserving federated learning for Alzheimer's disease classification using three-dimensional MRI data from the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI). Existing methodologies often suffer from unrealistic data partitioning, inadequate privacy guarantees, and insufficient benchmarking, limiting their practical deployment in healthcare. To address these gaps, this research proposes a novel site-aware data partitioning strategy that preserves institutional boundaries, reflecting real-world multi-institutional collaborations and data heterogeneity. Furthermore, an Adaptive Local Differential Privacy (ALDP) mechanism is introduced, dynamically adjusting privacy parameters based on training progression and parameter characteristics, thereby significantly improving the privacy-utility trade-off over traditional fixed-noise approaches. Systematic empirical evaluation across multiple client federations and privacy budgets demonstrated that advanced federated optimisation algorithms, particularly FedProx, could equal or surpass centralised training performance while ensuring rigorous privacy protection. Notably, ALDP achieved up to 80.4% accuracy in a two-client configuration, surpassing fixed-noise Local DP by 5-7 percentage points and demonstrating substantially greater training stability. The comprehensive ablation studies and benchmarking establish quantitative standards for privacy-preserving collaborative medical AI, providing practical guidelines for real-world deployment. This work thereby advances the state-of-the-art in federated learning for medical imaging, establishing both methodological foundations and empirical evidence necessary for future privacy-compliant AI adoption in healthcare.
2603.15901.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文探討了使用來自阿茲海默症神經影像倡議（ADNI）的三維MRI數據進行阿茲海默症分類的隱私保護聯邦學習。現有的方法論往往面臨不切實際的數據劃分、不充分的隱私保障和不足的基準測試，限制了它們在醫療保健中的實際應用。為了解決這些問題，本研究提出了一種新穎的考慮地點的數據劃分策略，保持機構邊界，反映現實世界中的多機構合作和數據異質性。此外，引入了一種自適應局部差分隱私（ALDP）機制，根據訓練進展和參數特徵動態調整隱私參數，從而顯著改善傳統固定噪聲方法的隱私效用權衡。在多個客戶聯盟和隱私預算下的系統性實證評估表明，先進的聯邦優化算法，特別是FedProx，可以達到或超過集中訓練的性能，同時確保嚴格的隱私保護。值得注意的是，ALDP在兩客戶配置中達到了高達80.4%的準確率，超過固定噪聲的局部差分隱私5-7個百分點，並顯示出顯著更大的訓練穩定性。全面的消融研究和基準測試建立了隱私保護協作醫療AI的定量標準，為現實世界的部署提供了實用指導。因此，這項工作推進了醫療影像聯邦學習的最先進技術，建立了未來醫療保健中隱私合規AI採用所需的方法論基礎和實證證據。
2603.15901.author: Tin Hoang et.al.
